-- Source claim excerpt: [Baseline lift for $\Psi_i$, $\Theta$, and $V_i$]\label{thm:baseline-lift} Assume $\alpha>3/4$. Let \begin{equation*} \Psi_i=I_i(\xi_i), \qquad \Theta=\Psi_1\Psi_2, \qquad V_i=I_i(\Theta). \end{equation*} Then the cutoff approximations converge almost surely in the path spaces \begin{equation}\label{eq:baseline-paths} \Psi_i\in C_T\mathcal{C}^{\alpha-3/2-}, \qquad \Theta\in C_T\mathcal{C}^{2\alpha-3-}, \qquad V_i\in C_T\mathcal{C}^{3\alpha-3-}, \qquad \partial_tV_i\in C_T\mathcal{C}^{2\alpha-3-}. \end{equation} In particular, $\Theta$ is a cross-Wick product without subtracting a scalar constant.
theorem thm_baseline_lift :
  alpha > 3/4 := by
  sorry

items.next_action: Replace paper-local axiom debt with definitions or proved local lemmas.
items.lean_statement:
-- Source claim excerpt: [Baseline cubic and quartic stochastic symbols]\label{prop:cubic-quartic-baseline} Assume $\alpha>3/4$. The cubic resonant symbols \begin{equation*} \Gamma_1=V_2\circ\Psi_1, \qquad \Gamma_2=V_1\circ\Psi_2 \end{equation*} admit canonical cutoff limits as stochastic distributions with baseline regularity \begin{equation}\label{eq:gamma-baseline} \Gamma_i\in C_T\mathcal{C}^{4\alpha-9/2-}. \end{equation} The nonzero-chaos part of $V_1V_2$ admits a canonical baseline lift in \begin{equation}\label{eq:vv-nonzero-baseline} (V_1V_2)^{\neq 0}\in C_T\mathcal{C}^{6\alpha-6-}. \end{equation} Moreover, the zero-chaos part of $V_1V_2$ is a deterministic scalar process; under the speed-separated double Volterra estimate its dyadic shell is $N^{6-7\alpha+}$, so it converges in $C_T$ for $\alpha>6/7$.
theorem prop_cubic_quartic_baseline :
  alpha > 3/4 ∧ alpha > 6/7 := by
  sorry

-- Source claim excerpt: [Completion outside the strong low--high region]\label{prop:mid-completion} Let the full paraproduct-resonant operator be decomposed into the strongly low--high region $|q|\le N^\theta$, the intermediate low--high region $N^\theta\lesssim |q|\ll N$, finitely many low shells, and the nonresonant paraproduct geometries. If the input has positive regularity $U\in C_TH^{s_1}$ with $s_1>0$, then the intermediate region contributes the dyadic shell \begin{equation}\label{eq:mid-shell-intro} N^{s_2+3-4\alpha-\theta s_1+\varepsilon}, \end{equation} and is therefore summable once \eqref{eq:operator-cond-main} holds. The remaining nonresonant and finite-shell pieces are bounded by standard paraproduct and finite-rank estimates.
theorem prop_mid_completion :
  s_1 > 0 := by
  sorry

-- Source claim excerpt: [Conditional deterministic closure]\label{thm:conditional-closure-intro} Assume the fractional periodic Klein--Gordon Strichartz package of Assumption \ref{ass:strichartz}. Assume that the enhanced stochastic data satisfy Theorem \ref{thm:baseline-lift}, Proposition \ref{prop:cubic-quartic-baseline}, Theorem \ref{thm:operator-main}, and Proposition \ref{prop:mid-completion}. Assume in addition that the pure stochastic forcing is available in the source norm \begin{equation}\label{eq:source-hyp-intro} F^V:=\Psi_1V_2+\Psi_2V_1+V_1V_2\in C_TH^{s_2-\alpha}, \end{equation} and that the parameters $(\alpha,s_1,s_2,\theta)$ satisfy the closure conditions listed in Definition \ref{def:admissible}. Then the paracontrolled system for the remainder variables $(X_1,X_2,Y_1,Y_2)$ is locally well posed in \begin{equation*} (X_T^{s_1})^2\times (Y_T^{s_2})^2. \end{equation*} The solution map is local...
theorem thm_conditional_closure_intro :
  ∃ x : ℝ, x = x := by
  sorry

-- Source claim excerpt: [Conditional convergence of the cutoff equations]\label{cor:conditional-cutoff} If the cutoff enhanced data converge in the norms required by Theorem \ref{thm:conditional-closure-intro} and are uniformly bounded on a common time interval, then the cutoff solutions of \eqref{eq:cutoff-system} converge to the paracontrolled solution. The limiting formulation contains no singular random-operator centering term.
theorem cor_conditional_cutoff :
  ∃ x : ℝ, x = x := by
  sorry

-- Source claim excerpt: [Status and emphasis]\label{rem:status-emphasis} The unconditional core of the paper is Theorem \ref{thm:baseline-lift}, Proposition \ref{prop:cubic-quartic-baseline}, Theorem \ref{thm:operator-main}, and Proposition \ref{prop:mid-completion}. The full stochastic Cauchy problem is conditional only because one still has to choose a closure route for the cubic forcing $F^V$ and insert an appropriate fractional periodic Klein--Gordon Strichartz theorem. In particular, the random-operator part is already complete at the level relevant to the no singular centering mechanism.
theorem rem_status_emphasis :
  ∃ x : ℝ, x = x := by
  sorry

-- Source claim excerpt: [Sharpness of the same-color shell]\label{prop:sharpness} There exist a fixed low output frequency $q$, a conic sector of the resonant shell, and a smooth time profile $w(q,t)$ supported only at that frequency such that the leading same-color deterministic contraction on the dyadic shell $|\ell|\sim N$ satisfies \begin{equation}\label{eq:sharpness} \|D_N^{i;j}w\|_{C_TH^{s_2-\alpha}}\gtrsim N^{3-4\alpha} \end{equation} up to strictly lower-order terms. Consequently $\alpha=3/4$ is logarithmically critical for the deterministic contraction channel.
theorem prop_sharpness :
  alpha = 3/4 := by
  sorry

-- Source claim excerpt: [Why $V_i$ is not inserted into the $L^2$ input slot] A basic consistency check is that the random operators act on $U_1$ and $U_2$, not on $V_1$ and $V_2$. In the fractional range $\alpha<1$, the first Picard objects $V_i$ have negative baseline regularity $3\alpha-3-$ and should be treated as part of the enhanced stochastic data, not as $L^2$ inputs of the random-operator theorem.
theorem remark_9 :
  alpha < 1 := by
  sorry

-- Source claim excerpt: [What is already complete here] At the level of canonical stochastic distributions, the cubic and quartic forcing terms are not the main difficulty. The already-integrated pieces enjoy additional phase geometry, and the lower-chaos contraction channels are finite. The real difficulty appears only if one insists on inserting the whole forcing into the conservative source norm $C_TH^{s_2-\alpha}$.
theorem remark_10 :
  ∃ x : ℝ, x = x := by
  sorry

-- Source claim excerpt: [Pathwise centered fluctuations]\label{thm:pathwise-fluct} Assume \begin{equation}\label{eq:a-neg} a:=6-8\alpha+2s_2+3\theta<0. \end{equation} Then the centered dyadic fluctuation series \begin{equation}\label{eq:fluct-series} B^{i;j,k}:=\sum_{N\text{ dyadic}}B_N^{i;j,k} \end{equation} converges almost surely in \begin{equation}\label{eq:fluct-op-space} \mathcal L\bigl(E_T,C_TH^{s_2-\alpha}\bigr). \end{equation} More precisely, for every sufficiently small $\kappa>0$ there is an almost surely finite random constant $C_\omega$ such that \begin{equation}\label{eq:fluct-dyadic-bound} \|B_N^{i;j,k}\|_{E_T\to C_TH^{s_2-\alpha}} \le C_\omega N^{a/2+\kappa} \end{equation} for all dyadic $N$.
theorem thm_pathwise_fluct :
  kappa > 0 := by
  sorry

-- Source claim excerpt: [Volterra oscillation estimate]\label{lem:volterra} Let $a\in C^1([0,T])$ and let $|\Phi|\gtrsim N^\alpha$. Then \begin{equation}\label{eq:volterra-est} \sup_{0\le t\le T} \Bigl|\int_0^t a(s)e^{\mathrm{i}(t-s)\Phi}\,ds\Bigr| \lesssim N^{-\alpha}\bigl(\|a\|_{L^\infty_T}+\|\partial_t a\|_{L^\infty_T}\bigr). \end{equation} The same bound holds with $\sin((t-s)\Phi)$ or $\cos((t-s)\Phi)$ in place of the exponential.
theorem lem_volterra :
  ∃ C : ℝ, 0 ≤ C := by
  sorry

-- Source claim excerpt: [Same-color deterministic contraction]\label{prop:det-contraction} Let $i\neq j$. Define \begin{equation}\label{eq:DN-operator} (\mathcal D_N^{i;j}w)_q(t)=\int_0^t D_N^{i;j}(q;t,s)\widehat w(q,s)\,ds. \end{equation} Then \begin{equation}\label{eq:DN-bound} \|\mathcal D_N^{i;j}w\|_{C_TH^{s_2-\alpha}} \lesssim_T \Bigl(N^{3-4\alpha}+N^{3-4\alpha+\theta(s_2+\varepsilon)}\Bigr) \bigl(\|w\|_{C_TL^2}+\|\partial_t w\|_{C_TH^{-\alpha-\varepsilon}}\bigr). \end{equation} Consequently $\sum_N\mathcal D_N^{i;j}$ converges in $\mathcal L(E_T,C_TH^{s_2-\alpha})$ whenever the second condition in \eqref{eq:operator-cond-main} holds.
theorem prop_det_contraction :
  ∃ C : ℝ, 0 ≤ C := by
  sorry

items.lean_statement:
-- Source claim excerpt: [No singular centering for mixed random operators]\label{thm:no-singular-centering} Assume \eqref{eq:operator-cond-main}. Then each strongly low--high mixed random operator has a cutoff limit as a pathwise bounded operator \begin{equation*} E_T\to C_TH^{s_2-\alpha}. \end{equation*} Cross-color blocks are automatically centered by independence. Same-color blocks decompose as \begin{equation*} T^{i;j,j}=\mathcal D^{i;j}+\mathcal B^{i;j,j}, \end{equation*} where $\mathcal D^{i;j}$ is a finite speed-separated deterministic Volterra multiplier and $\mathcal B^{i;j,j}$ is a centered pathwise random operator. No divergent random-operator counterterm is subtracted.
theorem thm_no_singular_centering :
  ∃ x : ℝ, x = x := by
  sorry

theorem def_admissible :
  ∃ alpha epsilon : ℝ, 0 < epsilon ∧ 0 < s1 ∧ s1 < s2 ∧ 0 < theta ∧ theta < 1 ∧ s2 < 4 * alpha - 3 - (3 / 2) * theta - epsilon ∧ 3 - 4 * alpha + theta * (s2 + epsilon) < 0 ∧ s1 < 2 * alpha - (3 / 2) - epsilon ∧ 3 / 2 - alpha + epsilon < s2 ∧ s2 < s1 + 2 * alpha - (3 / 2) - epsilon ∧ s2 ≤ s1 + alpha / 4 ∧ 0 < rho_V + s1 ∧ s2 - alpha < rho_V

-- Source claim excerpt: [Primitive route]\label{rem:primitive-route} The deterministic theorem above is stated in the conservative source formulation because it is the cleanest closed statement. For lowering the full SPDE threshold, however, the more natural route is to construct the primitives $G_i^V=I_i(F^V)$ directly in the solution space. This should exploit oscillatory geometry in the already-integrated cubic forcing that is invisible in the static source norm. The resulting enhanced formulation is deeper, because one must then control additional products involving $G_i^V$.
theorem rem_primitive_route :
  ∃ x : ℝ, x = x := by
  sorry

items.lean_statement:
def AutoCenteredFluctuationCondition (eps alpha s2 theta : ℝ) : Prop :=
    s2 < 4 * alpha - 3 - (3 / 2) * theta - eps

def AutoSameColorContractionCondition (eps alpha s2 theta : ℝ) : Prop :=
    3 - 4 * alpha + theta * (s2 + eps) < 0

def AutoNaiveLowHighMappingCondition (eps alpha s1 : ℝ) : Prop :=
    s1 < 2 * alpha - 3 / 2 - eps

def AutoBasicProductTheoryCondition (eps alpha s1 s2 : ℝ) : Prop :=
    3 / 2 - alpha + eps < s2 ∧
    s2 < s1 + 2 * alpha - 3 / 2 - eps

def AutoQuadraticStrichartzClosureCondition (alpha s1 s2 : ℝ) : Prop :=
    s2 ≤ s1 + alpha / 4

def AutoProductsViUjCondition (alpha s1 s2 rhoV : ℝ) : Prop :=
    rhoV + s1 > 0 ∧
    s2 - alpha < rhoV

def AutoAdmissibleFull (eps alpha s1 s2 theta rhoV : ℝ) : Prop :=
    0 < s1 ∧
    s1 < s2 ∧
    0 < theta ∧
    theta < 1 ∧
    AutoCenteredFluctuationCondition eps alpha s2 theta ∧
    AutoSameColorContractionCondition eps alpha s2 theta ∧
    AutoNaiveLowHighMappingCondition eps alpha s1 ∧
    AutoBasicProductTheoryCondition eps alpha s1 s2 ∧
    AutoQuadraticStrichartzClosureCondition alpha s1 s2 ∧
    AutoProductsViUjCondition alpha s1 s2 rhoV

theorem auto_def_admissible_iff (eps alpha s1 s2 theta rhoV : ℝ) :
    AutoAdmissibleFull eps alpha s1 s2 theta rhoV ↔
    (0 < s1 ∧
    s1 < s2 ∧
    0 < theta ∧
    theta < 1 ∧
    s2 < 4 * alpha - 3 - (3 / 2) * theta - eps ∧
    3 - 4 * alpha + theta * (s2 + eps) < 0 ∧
    s1 < 2 * alpha - 3 / 2 - eps ∧
    3 / 2 - alpha + eps < s2 ∧
    s2 < s1 + 2 * alpha - 3 / 2 - eps ∧
    s2 ≤ s1 + alpha / 4 ∧
    rhoV + s1 > 0 ∧
    s2 - alpha < rhoV)

items.next_action: No blocker detected for release promotion.
items.lean_statement:
def AutoRemark20ConditionRoles (eps alpha s1 s2 theta rhoV : ℝ) : Prop :=
    AutoCenteredFluctuationCondition eps alpha s2 theta ∧
    AutoSameColorContractionCondition eps alpha s2 theta ∧
    AutoNaiveLowHighMappingCondition eps alpha s1 ∧
    AutoBasicProductTheoryCondition eps alpha s1 s2 ∧
    AutoQuadraticStrichartzClosureCondition alpha s1 s2 ∧
    AutoProductsViUjCondition alpha s1 s2 rhoV

theorem auto_remark_20_condition_roles_iff (eps alpha s1 s2 theta rhoV : ℝ) :
    AutoRemark20ConditionRoles eps alpha s1 s2 theta rhoV ↔
    (s2 < 4 * alpha - 3 - (3 / 2) * theta - eps ∧
    3 - 4 * alpha + theta * (s2 + eps) < 0 ∧
    s1 < 2 * alpha - 3 / 2 - eps ∧
    (3 / 2 - alpha + eps < s2 ∧ s2 < s1 + 2 * alpha - 3 / 2 - eps) ∧
    s2 ≤ s1 + alpha / 4 ∧
    (rhoV + s1 > 0 ∧ s2 - alpha < rhoV))
